body.content: Academies

Academies are a prominent part of factions across SCP:Roleplay
From a simple bootcamps lasting an hour, to long-winded courses comprising of multiple phases, the News Team wanted to see what factions with Academies had to think about them.

We've invited Command members from **Resh-1**, **Alpha-1 **and **ADC-2** from Site: Noctum to give their opinions on their respective academies
If the article is too long, you may read the summary at the bottom
What do you believe makes a good academy?
**__*Resh-1 P.A. Director | Roman:*__**

"A good academy **instructor** team, good **communication** between academy and trainee, and a good **leadership** overseeing the academy. Alongside __phases that push someone to the limit that they can reach__, then attempting to help them improve"
**__*Alpha-1 P.A. Director | Matte:*__**

"A good academy, specially for more elite factions like Alpha-1, is like not to be a Marines Boot Camp. I mean at the end of the day __it is just Roblox__, but we also look for some of the __*best SCP:RP pvpers and players*__. So personally i'd say making sure to **not have too many phases**, where the academy members get overwhelmed, instead just have an academy with like **3-4 phases** where each phase __tells much__ about the person trying to join your faction."
Do you believe that academies are useful in a way? (and should they be implemented throughout all factions?)
***__ADC-2 Guardsmen Overseer | Alexander S. / Richard H:__***

"Academies are really useful for those who don't yet understand the **__basic concept of factions__**, perhaps due to a lack of experience or internal factional selection. As they progress through the academy, they gain a **deeper understanding** of the purpose of factions and the qualities necessary to advance in their careers and share their expertise with other recruits.
***__Resh-1 P.A. Director | Roman:__***

"Academies are useful because it allows us to identify the people with dedication and skill, out of the people who sit on their ass all day and are incapable of listening to orders, alongside being bad at the game (pvp and rp wise)."
[Add on by Academy Overseer | Omnissiah] "having members that are more experienced than factions with no academy means that they are less likely to get us punished for basic rule breaking (RK etc.)"
***__Alpha-1 P.A. Director | Matte:__***

"I think our academy is useful in many ways, as we get to know beforehand who is worth our time, and who will actually be able to provide for the faction. I wouldn't say an academy like ours should be implemented in EVERY faction, but having some kind of entry process would be good, to get to know your future faction members."
Should all academies adopt XXX's system of phases?
***__Alpha-1 P.A. Director | Matte:__***

"I would not say ALL academies, i mean at the end of the day it depends how serious you want to take it, and in what ways you want to know who you are hiring. But i'd say that Alpha-1's academy system should be a base of kind of what your academy needs."
***__Resh-1 P.A. Director | Roman:__***

"No, of course not. I personally think that every academy should be different, so that people were not all copy-paste's of each other. If all academies have the same phases, we'd never be better than one another, thus sort of ruining the competition of ' who is better '."
[To ADC-2] What are some potential downsides?
***__ADC-2 Guardsmen Overseer | Alexander S. / Richard H:__***

"Despite all the advantages, there are still some disadvantages. I would like to point out that, based on my personal experience, each recruit has their own unique personality and experience in role-playing games, which makes it difficult to transfer knowledge and skills to them. This is because each recruit requires different amounts of time and attention, which can slow down the progress of the academy and hinder their advancement in the organization. In some cases, this can lead to a loss of interest and recruitment into factions with lower requirements."
Summary
